key_sentences.chinese: 你最近越来越胖了，要注意健康啊。
key_sentences.pinyin: Nǐ zuì jìn yuè lái yuè pàng le, yào zhù yì jiàn kāng a.
key_sentences.english: You've been getting fatter and fatter lately; you need to watch your health.

key_sentences.chinese: 我知道，我打算开始锻炼。
key_sentences.pinyin: Wǒ zhī dào, wǒ dǎ suàn kāi shǐ duàn liàn.
key_sentences.english: I know; I'm planning to start exercising.

key_sentences.chinese: 你越锻炼，体重越容易减下来。
key_sentences.pinyin: Nǐ yuè duàn liàn, tǐ zhòng yuè róng yì jiǎn xià lái.
key_sentences.english: The more you exercise, the easier it is to lose weight.

key_sentences.chinese: 她在节食减肥，现在越来越瘦了。
key_sentences.pinyin: Tā zài jié shí jiǎn féi, xiàn zài yuè lái yuè shòu le.
key_sentences.english: She's been dieting to lose weight; now she's getting thinner and thinner.

key_sentences.chinese: 比较健康的生活方式是多运动，少吃油腻的食物。
key_sentences.pinyin: Bǐ jiào jiàn kāng de shēng huó fāng shì shì duō yùn dòng, shǎo chī yóu nì de shí wù.
key_sentences.english: A relatively healthy lifestyle is to exercise more and eat less greasy food.

key_sentences.chinese: 你怎么这么瘦，要注意多吃一点啊。
key_sentences.pinyin: Nǐ zěn me zhè me shòu, yào zhù yì duō chī yì diǎn a.
key_sentences.english: How are you so thin? You need to make sure you eat more.

grammar_points.title: 越来越+Adj — More and More
grammar_points.explanation: Subject + 越来越 + Adjective/Verb (+ 了) expresses a gradual increase or worsening of a state. 越来越胖了 = getting fatter and fatter. 越来越好了 = getting better and better. 了 is often added at the end to signal the change is ongoing. This structure cannot be used with a comparison — it means an absolute, progressive change.

examples.pinyin: Tiān qì yuè lái yuè rè le.
examples.english: The weather is getting hotter and hotter.

examples.pinyin: Tā de shēn tǐ yuè lái yuè hǎo le.
examples.english: His body condition is getting better and better.

exercises.question: How do you say 'She's getting thinner and thinner'?
option_pinyin: Tā yuè lái yuè shòu le.
Tā shòu yuè lái yuè le.
Tā yuè shòu yuè lái yuè.
Yuè lái yuè tā shòu le.
exercises.correct_pinyin: Tā yuè lái yuè shòu le.

exercises.question: What is the role of 了 at the end of 越来越胖了?
exercises.correct: It signals the change is ongoing
options: It signals the change is ongoing
It makes the sentence a question

exercises.question: Fill in: 他最近锻炼___了，体重___来___好了。(He's been exercising more lately; his weight is getting better and better.)
Which sentence means 'It's getting colder and colder'?

mini_exercises.question: What does 越来越 + Adjective express?
mini_exercises.correct: A gradual increase of a quality (more and more...)
options: A gradual increase of a quality (more and more...)

mini_exercises.question: Fill in: The weather is getting hotter and hotter.
mini_exercises.question_english: The weather is getting hotter and hotter.

mini_exercises.question: Which sentence is correct?
option_pinyin: tā yuè lái yuè piào liang le
tā piào liang yuè lái yuè le
yuè lái yuè tā piào liang le
tā le yuè lái yuè piào liang

grammar_points.title: 越…越… — The More…The More…
grammar_points.explanation: Subject + 越 + V/Adj + 越 + V/Adj expresses a proportional relationship: as one thing increases, so does another. 越吃越胖 = The more you eat, the fatter you get. 越学越有意思 = The more you study, the more interesting it gets. The subject can be split: 你越锻炼，你越健康. This pattern emphasises cause and effect.

examples.pinyin: Yuè chī yuè pàng, yuè bú dòng yuè lǎn.
examples.english: The more you eat, the fatter you get; the less you move, the lazier you become.

examples.chinese: 你越坚持锻炼，身体越健康。
examples.pinyin: Nǐ yuè jiān chí duàn liàn, shēn tǐ yuè jiàn kāng.
examples.english: The more you persist in exercising, the healthier your body gets.

exercises.question: What does 越…越… express?
exercises.correct: A proportional relationship: as one thing increases, so does another
options: A proportional relationship: as one thing increases, so does another

exercises.question: How do you say 'The more you study, the smarter you get'?
option_pinyin: Yuè lái yuè xué cōng míng.

exercises.question: Fill in: 你___锻炼，体重___容易减下来。(The more you exercise, the easier it is to lose weight.)
What is the difference between 越来越 and 越…越…?
exercises.correct: 越来越 = single progressive change; 越…越… = two linked changes
options: 越来越 = single progressive change; 越…越… = two linked changes
越来越 is for adjectives only; 越…越… is for verbs only

exercises.question: Which sentence uses 越…越… correctly?

mini_exercises.question: Fill in: The more you eat, the fatter you get.
mini_exercises.question_english: The more you eat, the fatter you get.

grammar_points.title: 比较 — Relatively / Comparatively
grammar_points.explanation: 比较 + Adjective means 'relatively/rather + adjective'. 比较健康 = relatively healthy. It softens the statement compared to 很 (very) or 非常 (extremely) — it means 'compared to other things/people, it is X'. 比较 can also be used as a verb meaning 'to compare': 我们来比较一下 = Let's do a comparison.

examples.chinese: 这个减肥方法比较健康。
examples.pinyin: Zhè gè jiǎn féi fāng fǎ bǐ jiào jiàn kāng.
examples.english: This weight-loss method is relatively healthy.

examples.chinese: 她最近比较注意饮食，也坚持锻炼。
examples.pinyin: Tā zuì jìn bǐ jiào zhù yì yǐn shí, yě jiān chí duàn liàn.
examples.english: She has been paying relatively more attention to her diet lately and also keeps exercising.

exercises.question: What does 比较 mean before an adjective?
How does 比较 differ from 很?
exercises.correct: 比较 means 'relatively'; 很 means 'very' — 比较 is softer
options: 比较 means 'relatively'; 很 means 'very' — 比较 is softer
They mean exactly the same thing
比较 is stronger than 很
比较 is only used in formal writing

exercises.question: How do you say 'This exercise method is relatively healthy'?
exercises.correct: 这个锻炼方法比较健康。
options: 这个锻炼方法比较健康。
这个锻炼方法很比较健康。
比较这个锻炼方法健康。
这个锻炼方法健康比较。

lines.speaker: A
lines.chinese: 加缪，你最近越来越胖了，有注意到吗？
lines.pinyin: Jiā Miù, nǐ zuì jìn yuè lái yuè pàng le, yǒu zhù yì dào ma?
lines.english: Camus, you've been getting fatter and fatter lately — have you noticed?

lines.speaker: B
lines.chinese: 哎，我知道，最近吃太多了，而且没有锻炼。
lines.pinyin: Āi, wǒ zhī dào, zuì jìn chī tài duō le, ér qiě méi yǒu duàn liàn.
lines.english: Ah, I know — I've been eating too much lately, and I haven't been exercising either.

lines.speaker: A
lines.chinese: 你越吃越胖，越不动越懒，要开始坚持锻炼了。
lines.pinyin: Nǐ yuè chī yuè pàng, yuè bú dòng yuè lǎn, yào kāi shǐ jiān chí duàn liàn le.
lines.english: The more you eat, the fatter you get; the less you move, the lazier you become — you need to start exercising consistently.

lines.speaker: B
lines.chinese: 说得对。我打算开始节食减肥，也要每天锻炼。
lines.pinyin: Shuō de duì. Wǒ dǎ suàn kāi shǐ jié shí jiǎn féi, yě yào měi tiān duàn liàn.
lines.english: You're right. I plan to start dieting to lose weight, and also exercise every day.

lines.speaker: A
lines.chinese: 比较健康的方法是多锻炼，少节食，这样体重慢慢就减下来了。
lines.pinyin: Bǐ jiào jiàn kāng de fāng fǎ shì duō duàn liàn, shǎo jié shí, zhè yàng tǐ zhòng màn màn jiù jiǎn xià lái le.
lines.english: The relatively healthier approach is to exercise more and diet less — that way your weight will slowly come down.

lines.speaker: B
lines.chinese: 好，我从明天开始减肥！你帮我监督一下吧。
lines.pinyin: Hǎo, wǒ cóng míng tiān kāi shǐ jiǎn féi ! Nǐ bāng wǒ jiān dū yí xià ba.
lines.english: OK, I'll start dieting from tomorrow! Help keep an eye on me.

lines.speaker: A
lines.chinese: 苏轼，你怎么越来越瘦了，有在减肥吗？
lines.pinyin: Sū Shì, nǐ zěn me yuè lái yuè shòu le, yǒu zài jiǎn féi ma?
lines.english: Su Shi, how are you getting thinner and thinner? Are you losing weight?

lines.speaker: B
lines.chinese: 是啊，我最近坚持每天锻炼，也比较注意饮食。
lines.pinyin: Shì a, wǒ zuì jìn jiān chí měi tiān duàn liàn, yě bǐ jiào zhù yì yǐn shí.
lines.english: Yes! I've been consistently exercising every day lately, and paying relatively more attention to my diet.

lines.speaker: A
lines.chinese: 你越锻炼越健康，感觉精神也越来越好了！
lines.pinyin: Nǐ yuè duàn liàn yuè jiàn kāng, gǎn jué jīng shén yě yuè lái yuè hǎo le!
lines.english: The more you exercise, the healthier you get; your energy also seems to be getting better and better!

lines.speaker: B
lines.chinese: 谢谢！不过你这么瘦，要多吃一点，别节食了。
lines.pinyin: Xiè xiè! Bú guò nǐ zhè me shòu, yào duō chī yì diǎn, bié jié shí le.
lines.english: Thank you! But you're so slim — you should eat more, don't diet.

lines.speaker: A
lines.chinese: 哈哈，我也想坚持锻炼，这样比较健康！
lines.pinyin: Hā hā, wǒ yě xiǎng jiān chí duàn liàn, zhè yàng bǐ jiào jiàn kāng!
lines.english: Haha, I also want to keep exercising consistently — that's relatively healthier!
